rationale.root_cause: At the FLAW site (around line 42), memcpy uses sizeof(*structCharVoid) instead of sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst), copying the whole struct into a 16-byte array and overwriting adjacent fields. This corrupts voidSecond/voidThird and leads to a crash when the corrupted pointer is used.
rationale.local_fix: Change the memcpy length to sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst) and keep the explicit null terminator. This copies only the destination field’s size and prevents overwriting the pointers.
rationale.global_fix: Introduce a bounded field-copy helper or typed buffer wrapper that derives the destination size (e.g., macro/function taking an array and using sizeof dest) to prevent confusing struct size with field size. Such an API should reject pointer destinations and enforce correct bounds automatically.
